user_query:
I'm a network administrator helping our on-site health kiosks team validate and summarize anonymized vital-sign exports from two clinic sites. Please build a small, local-only workflow that reads all CSV files under input/clinics/ recursively, normalizes their columns using the provided mapping in scripts/etl_template.py, applies the vital thresholds in config/vitals_thresholds.yaml, and writes structured results to a new results/ directory. Constraints and deliverables:

Inputs to use:
- Column canonicalization: scripts/etl_template.py (use COLUMN_MAP to map site-specific column names to the canonical names listed in that file; do not hardcode your own names).
- Vital thresholds: config/vitals_thresholds.yaml (treat values strictly outside the inclusive [min, max] range as abnormal; inclusive boundaries are normal).
- Data files: all *.csv found anywhere under input/clinics/ (traverse subdirectories; do not hardcode specific filenames). Timestamps are in UTC (either with a trailing Z or +00:00).

Canonical columns required after mapping (must be present per row to evaluate): heart_rate, systolic_bp, diastolic_bp, temperature_c, timestamp, site. If any of these are missing in a file after mapping, flag it in the audit (see below).

Processing rules:
- Recursively inspect files and directories under input/clinics/ to discover all CSVs.
- Inspect scripts/etl_template.py to apply the provided COLUMN_MAP for canonical field names, and treat timestamp/time as the canonical timestamp, and clinic_id/site as the canonical site.
- Inspect config/vitals_thresholds.yaml and use the min/max for each vital to determine abnormal readings. Consider a row "abnormal_any" if any of the four vitals is outside the inclusive [min, max] range. Skip rows with non-numeric vital values when computing abnormality but count them as invalid_rows in the audit.
- Parse timestamps as UTC and compute ISO week and year from the timestamp for aggregation.

Write the following outputs to the local workspace (create results/ if it does not exist):
1) results/summary.csv with columns exactly: site,year,iso_week,total_readings,abnormal_any,abnormal_heart_rate,abnormal_systolic_bp,abnormal_diastolic_bp,abnormal_temperature_c. Each row summarizes one site-week across all files processed.
2) results/abnormal_events.jsonl where each line is a JSON object for one abnormal reading with fields: site, timestamp, heart_rate, systolic_bp, diastolic_bp, temperature_c, abnormal_fields (array naming which of the four vitals were out of range for that row), and source_file (relative path to the CSV the record came from).
3) results/schema_audit.csv with columns: file_path,total_rows,invalid_rows,columns_found,unmapped_columns,missing_canonical_columns. List columns_found as a comma-separated list of the original column names in that file; unmapped_columns as a comma-separated list of input columns that did not appear in COLUMN_MAP; missing_canonical_columns as a comma-separated list of any canonical columns that could not be produced for that file.

Please do not alter the input files. Make sure the three outputs reflect all input files under input/clinics/. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
